---
title: "UIAO SSOT Roster Schema"
subtitle: "Schema Reference · ssot-roster.schema.json"
date: 2026-05-18
---

::: {.callout-note}
## Developer Reference

This is a UIAO JSON Schema, rendered as a developer-reference page per
[ADR-072](../../adr/adr-072-canon-publication-policy.qmd).

- **Source:** [`src/uiao/schemas/ssot-roster/ssot-roster.schema.json`](../../../src/uiao/schemas/ssot-roster/ssot-roster.schema.json)
- **Dialect:** `http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#`
- **`$id`:** `https://uiao.gov/schemas/ssot-roster/ssot-roster.schema.json`
:::

## Description

JSON Schema for **SSOT (single-source-of-truth) roster** documents. Each roster declares, for one data domain, which instance is authoritative and which instances are canonically demoted in favor of it. The substrate's drift engine (the proposed `SSOTContentionEvaluator`) reads these rosters to evaluate `DRIFT-SSOT-CONTENTION` findings per [ADR-074](../../adr/adr-074-drift-ssot-contention.qmd).

Companion to the **MS SQL Estate Rationalization Process spec** (§3 SSOT Designation, §7.2 `DRIFT-SSOT-CONTENTION` detection mechanism). The spec catalyzed the schema; the schema applies to any data domain that the rationalization process framework covers (HR, Finance, Procurement, Logistics, FOIA, etc.).

One roster per (domain, domain_scope) pair. Rosters are canon-anchored YAML files under `src/uiao/canon/data/<adapter-family>/ssot-roster.yaml`.

## Top-Level Properties

| Property | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|:---:|---|
| `schema-version` | string (semver) | ✓ | Roster document schema version. |
| `domain` | string (pattern: `^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_-]*$`) | ✓ | Canonical data-domain identifier from the seed catalog or agency extension. |
| `domain_scope` | string |   | Optional sub-domain qualifier when one domain splits across mission boundaries. |
| `ratification_date` | string (date) | ✓ | ISO 8601 date the governance board ratified this roster. |
| `governance_review_pr` | string |   | PR reference for audit traceability (required for FedRAMP audit chain). |
| `authoritative_instance` | `$ref: AuthoritativeInstance` | ✓ | The single SSOT instance. |
| `demoted_instances` | array&lt;`$ref: DemotedInstance`&gt; | ✓ | Instances canonically demoted in favor of the SSOT. |
| `remediation_window_days` | integer (1–365, default 30) |   | Default window before contention findings escalate P2 → P1. |
| `remediation_events` | array&lt;`$ref: RemediationEvent`&gt; |   | Append-only audit log of closed findings. |
| `notes` | string |   | Free-text notes. |

## `AuthoritativeInstance` Properties

| Property | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|:---:|---|
| `identifier` | string | ✓ | Stable identifier. Format matches the discovering adapter's id convention (e.g., `ad-spn::host:port`, ARM resource id, `workday::tenant-id`). |
| `boundary_classification` | enum | ✓ | Governance boundary (drives boundary-fit eligibility per rationalization spec §4.2): `gcc-moderate`, `gcc-high`, `dod-il4`, `dod-il5`, or named commercial-exception values. |
| `selection_rationale` | string | ✓ | Governance-board rationale referencing the spec §3 weighted-criteria scoring outcome. |
| `discovery_source` | string |   | Conformance adapter id surfacing this instance. |

## `DemotedInstance` Properties

| Property | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|:---:|---|
| `identifier` | string | ✓ | Stable identifier (same format conventions as authoritative). |
| `demotion_date` | string (date) | ✓ | ISO 8601 date marked demoted. |
| `cutover_window` | object |   | `planned_start` + `planned_end` (date-time). Findings during this window emit at P3 rather than P2. |
| `cache_eligible_columns` | array&lt;`table.column`&gt; |   | Allowlist of write-permitted columns (downstream caches). Pattern: `schema.table.column` or `table.column`. Writes outside this list trigger `DRIFT-SSOT-CONTENTION`. |
| `retirement_date` | date \| null |   | Scheduled decommission date; null until scheduled. |
| `status` | enum | ✓ | `demoted`, `cutover-in-progress`, `cutover-complete`, `retired`. |
| `remediation_window_days` | integer |   | Per-instance override of the roster-level value. |
| `discovery_source` | string |   | Conformance adapter id. |

## `RemediationEvent` Properties

| Property | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|:---:|---|
| `finding_id` | string | ✓ | The `DriftFinding` identifier the remediation closes. |
| `decision` | enum | ✓ | `re-ratify`, `re-direct`, or `retire` (per ADR-074 §5 and spec §7.3). |
| `decision_date` | string (date) | ✓ | ISO 8601 date the governance steward recorded the decision. |
| `decision_pr` | string | ✓ when `decision == "re-ratify"` | PR reference that landed the decision. Required when the roster mutates. |
| `notes` | string |   | Free-text. |

## Relationship to other canon

| Canon | Relationship |
|---|---|
| [ADR-074](../../adr/adr-074-drift-ssot-contention.qmd) | Defines `DRIFT-SSOT-CONTENTION` — the drift class this roster's rules govern. |
| MS SQL Estate Rationalization Process (spec) | Catalyzing use case. Spec §3 defines the SSOT designation process; spec §7.2 defines the detection mechanism the roster supports. |
| `drift-engine-config.schema.json` | Carries the `drift_class` enum that includes `DRIFT-SSOT-CONTENTION`. The roster is the *baseline* the engine evaluates against. |
| `adapter-registry.yaml` | The `discovery_source` field on each instance pins to a registered adapter id. |

## Schema location and validation

- Source: [`src/uiao/schemas/ssot-roster/ssot-roster.schema.json`](../../../src/uiao/schemas/ssot-roster/ssot-roster.schema.json)
- Validated by the substrate's `Validate canon documents against schemas` CI job for any roster under `src/uiao/canon/data/**/ssot-roster.yaml`.

The first production roster is **not** part of this PR — it lands per the rationalization spec §9.6-9.8 sequencing, after the seed reference-data + domain-catalog canon data files arrive.
